ideas.intro: Ideas for projects, activities, books, games, and more for students with a high level of interest.

items.content:
[ ] Watch some of the bay and harbour [webcams](https://tourismtofino.com/plan-your-trip/weather-webcams/) located in Tofino on Vancouver Island. 
[ ] Try these [classic Canadian recipes](https://canadianfoodfocus.org/in-your-kitchen/classic-canadian-dishes/) from the provinces you are studying. 
[ ] View arctic wildlife in[ this slideshow ](https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/ccf0ce17869b494683ec440b7d58ace4)from Canadian Geographic.

[ ] [View this map ](https://canadaoceanmap.ca/#)of Canada's ocean and freshwater system. 
[ ] [This video](https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/95c8dfc9d1584d459e7e6c6f84422673) looks at how the cycle of the seasons affects the way we connect with the land, through the lenses of both the Métis and the Woodland Cree Nations. 
[ ] [Watch a trip](https://youtu.be/OH3vU2WP89U?si=cxw9-IskQQ5-9gmO) through the Canadian Rockies with this family.

[ ] [Enjoy this video](https://youtu.be/1Lnw6tsDg3w?si=-cNHN6piKAhIlbH_) about Niagara Falls. 
[ ] [Learn some fun facts ](https://youtu.be/MhTxCncUgC4?si=qC-2PI80fiw5AoEk)about Quebec. 
[ ] Use what you've learned about flags to [create your own flag](https://justfamilyfun.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/Flag-Outlines.pdf) for your family, team, or town.
